problem.title: Design TinyURL
problem.opening_prompt: Design a URL shortening service like TinyURL. Users submit long URLs and get short links back. Take it from there.

phases.name: Phase 1: Requirements
phases.exit_gate: Candidate defines system boundaries and traffic targets.

subtopics.description: Core features like custom slugs and click tracking.
Exclusions like user auth or frontend UI.
Setting explicit p99 latency and availability targets.

phases.name: Phase 2: High Level Design
phases.exit_gate: All core system components are laid out and connected.

subtopics.description: Separation of link creation vs redirection.
Tradeoffs between HTTP status codes regarding caching and analytics.
Choosing a DB optimized for heavy reads and key-value lookups.
Data model for storing short-to-long URL mappings and metadata.
Where the cache sits in the architecture.

phases.name: Phase 3: Deep Dive
phases.exit_gate: Candidate successfully solves the system's primary bottlenecks.

subtopics.description: Math behind hash collisions (MD5 vs Base62).
Pre-generating IDs at scale without DB lock bottlenecks (e.g., Key Generation Service).
How data is sharded (hash vs range) and rebalanced under load.
Handling sudden, massive traffic spikes on un-cached viral links.

phases.name: Phase 4: Scale and Operations
phases.exit_gate: Candidate proves the system survives failures and abuse.

subtopics.description: Behavior when the primary database cluster goes down.
Handling malicious scripts trying to consume all possible short slugs.
Key metrics to trigger alerts (e.g., cache hit rate, redirect latency).
